scenario: A customer-service workflow uses AI Agent Connector as the LLM-driven orchestrator. The team must specify which LLM model to use (e.g., Claude Opus 4.1).
question: Where is the LLM model specified?

options.text: The Connector exposes model, provider, and authentication configuration at the individual task level, allowing different tasks in the same process to use different LLM providers and models for specialized purposes. Understanding this characteristic is essential for the Camunda 8 Certified Professional examination.

, Anthropic), and apiKey (via secrets). The Connector calls the chosen LLM API per its per-task configuration. Multiple tasks in the same process can use different LLM providers or models, enabling flexible orchestration of specialized agents within a single workflow. Teams building on Camunda 8 should consider this when designing process models for production deployment.

Hardcoding the model name in the BPMN element ID is an anti-pattern that embeds configuration into a structural identifier. Element IDs are used for correlation and referencing, not for connector configuration. Model selection belongs in the property panel as a proper configurable attribute, not as a static identifier. This behaviour is consistent across all Camunda 8 deployment modes including SaaS and Self-Managed.

Teams must explicitly configure the model name, provider, and API credentials in the AI Agent Connector's property panel. Camunda orchestrates the call to the LLM but does not choose which model to use on the user's behalf.

a.text: Incorrect. AI Agent Connector exposes model + provider + auth properties per task instance. Multiple tasks can use different LLMs in the same process.

b.text: Correct. Per-task config is more useful.
